metric_value: Worried; fear of inward integration focus
notes: HP and Compaq customers told Aberdeen they worried the combined company would focus inwardly.
HP printers and Compaq storage were identified as least-redundant product lines.
Overlapping Intel/Windows server lines identified as a key integration challenge.

metric_value: Both offer Unix enterprise server products
notes: Both companies had overlapping Unix server product lines.
Common Itanium architecture described as shared future direction.

metric_value: Requires sustained outward customer focus for 2+ years
notes: Kastner predicts merger succeeds only with outward customer focus during 2-year integration.
Kastner warns Dell, IBM, and Sun will benefit if HP-Compaq loses customer focus.

metric_value: Combined HP services comparable to IBM Global Services
notes: Post-merger services org described as able to compete with IBM Global Services.

metric_value: Immediate customer communications with articulated differentiation
notes: Aberdeen recommends immediate steps for customer communication even before merger completion.

metric_value: Invest in retraining sales force; better than cutting costs
notes: Kastner recommends quality programs and sales force retraining as merger success prescription.

metric_value: Enterprise sales vs. channel/dealer vs. consumer retail balance critical
notes: Delicate multi-channel balance must be carefully maintained to avoid conflicts.
Kastner argues merger message must be 'better not more' to succeed with customers.

metric_value: No rush by customers to other suppliers at time of announcement
notes: Customer base ranges from fanatical to somewhat satisfied; no immediate defection.

metric_value: Clarity of messages largely absent for past 2 years
notes: Kastner identifies marketing execution as historically weak and critical to address.

metric_value: Both engineering organizations top-notch; capable of innovation
notes: Kastner affirms both HP and Compaq engineering teams as high quality.

metric_value: Sun named alongside Dell and IBM
notes: Sun Microsystems identified as competitive winner if HP-Compaq integration fails.
